reviews.review_text:
Drummond Cove Holiday Caravan Park has so much to offer,  great rooms,  stunning views, plenty of parking, and green lawn caravan parking bays.
Just wish we had found this gem earlier,  and stayed longer.

We loved our overnight stop here.
Toilets and showers were lovely and clean, and we had a lovely spot to park our van and could see the ocean, which was really nice after a day on the road.

Staying here now.
Its a hidden gem.
Wonderful grounds, carefully thought out cabin designs, comfy beds, and beautiful veiws. Enough space for kids to ride their bikes. 10 or so mins out of town.

Stayed at Drummond Cove Holiday Park and really enjoyed it—a great place to relax with beautiful ocean views. It’s just a short drive from Geraldton and feels peaceful, surrounded by nature and open space.

It’s a good place to visit, especially for families. There’s a kids’ playground and pool area, so children have space to enjoy and play. The park is well set up with clean toilets, laundry, BBQ areas, and basic shop facilities, which makes the stay comfortable.

The accommodation options are flexible too—cabins, powered and unpowered sites—so it suits both travellers and campers.

The staff are very helpful and friendly, always ready to assist and make your stay comfortable. They greeted us warmly on arrival, provided helpful local tips, ensured quick check-in, and were always approachable whenever we needed support. The sunset views are absolutely beautiful, perfect for relaxing evenings by the coast.

Overall, it’s a great stopover spot—quiet, scenic, and well-maintained. Definitely worth visiting.

Great park with clean amenities. They put on a movie and activities for kids over Easter. Friendly staff and very helpful.

Great view of the ocean from the park.

The location was absolutely beautiful — we were lucky enough to see both an amazing sunset and sunrise, which made the stay even more special. The staff were fantastic as well, so friendly and helpful throughout our visit. We had a wonderful experience and would definitely recommend staying here!
Wow what a great place to stay pet friendly staff are amazing and amenities are spotless all ways clean highly recommended place to be
Best park we've been to.  The Indigenous corporation which runs it does a fantastic job.  The people are friendly. Sites are huge. View is unbeatable.  Thanks guys & girls!

Great stay, booked a room and the guests wanted to extend so instead of apologising and not letting us stay they upgraded us for no fee! Even with pooches this caravan park is an absolute must stay and will never be going anywhere else again. Thanks Drummonds for a lovely park

Wunderschöne Lage mit Meerblick.
Toiletten und Duschen sind sauber.
Etwas gestört hat uns, dass die Küche noch im Bau war und deshalb nicht benutzt werden konnte, jedoch wurde der volle Preis verlangt.
Die nette Frau an der Rezeption ist sehr gesprächig.
Lovely campsite overlooking the coast. The shower/toilet/laundry facilities were so clean and worked well and the staff were friendly - would recommend for camping.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We have absolutely loved our stay here. The rooms have been significantly updated since the last time I stayed several years ago, and the difference is amazing. The cleanliness is the best I have experienced anywhere, everything felt fresh, spotless, and thoughtfully set up.
So much care has gone into the layout and details, from hooks for wet towels to the quality of the furniture, bedding, and towels. The towels, cloths, and soaps were beautifully arranged, and the bottled water waiting in the fridge was such a thoughtful touch. Walking into the room to find the air conditioning already on was wonderful. A cool, clean, and welcoming space from the moment we arrived.
The grounds are just as impressive, with plenty of beautiful grassed areas for the kids to run and play, playgrounds, and a gorgeous pool that the whole family can enjoy. The view of the ocean from our room is absolutely out of this world, and the coastal breeze in the evening is divine. The villas are also very well spaced, giving a wonderful sense of privacy.
The customer service and helpful information we received were friendly, welcoming, and greatly appreciated.
We are so glad to have found somewhere in Geraldton that is affordable while still offering such high quality, comfort, and attention to detail. We would 100% recommend this accommodation to everyone and will definitely be staying again. 👏☺️
Heard so much about this caravan park in Geraldton want to stay there if possible

Excellent value for money.
My cabin had an ocean view, and for the moderately fit tourist, there is a walking trail to the beach.
Note on the walking trail:
Those with mobility issues are advised not to take this trail as it has steep sections and gravelly sections with some potential to slip.

Drummond Cove Holiday Park not only had a tranquil location next to the beach and great amenities, but they also helped us out greatly when we were in a pickle for accommodation and storage after a car crash, cheers Drummond Cove Holiday Park you ended up being one of the best places we stayed at of our trip, and we if we are staying in Geraldton again we will definitely book here (hopefully under better circumstances lol)!
Great caravan park, staff are great. Rustic feel, amenities clean, pool, camp kitchen and dump point.
Nice park, very clean ammenities. Lovely staff. Large reno on the way during off season, more electricity and plumbing I think. This place is going to be the place to book!
Staying in my mobile home at this caravan Park.. suits me very well...
Was awesome 👍
Nic clean and good cabins renovated
Good services
Cosy spot while travelling
Was a quiet pleasant stay  I highly recommend it very nice
Nice place to visit .
Great staff and hotel
Awesome
Nice and cosy place to live, Very nice service
Great, friendly and beautiful.
Throughly enjoyed our stay, caravan sites are nice and large, ablutions clean.
Great little park. Staff were very good. Clean tidy amenities.
Miserable, greedy. You're not getting any more of my money.

Rooms are rubbish, filth in humane almost. The stench is horrible. From showers you can't fit in and flooding the bathroom, to a stove top where the range hood is covered in 30 year old grease and oil. Bed bugs and a lounge that is stained that bad they have thrown a cover over top of and smells that bad it makes you sick to sit inside you spend the whole time outside. The floor has been relined but you still fall through the holes in the floor underneath. Should be shut down. Better living conditions in a detention centre I'm sure.
Great views, nice spot in gero

Stayed two nights. Very friendly and helpful staff. Had to upgrade to a cabin due to the wind and road noise being too loud for tent camping. The cabin was modern with a great view. Thanks for a lovely stay!

Cabin is lovely. Renovated really nice. Brand new everything from kitchen, bathroom to bedroom. Towels blankets, curtains, fridge, you name it and it's new. Staff are friendly. Rates are great. Beautiful ocean views. We've stayed in lots of places in Geraldton and this is the best. We're from Mt Magnet and come into Geraldton quite regular so here is where we will stay from now on.

Very disappointed. Web site says closing time 7pm. Arrived 6:30pm. Closed sign on the door. Closes 5pm
Maybe update the Web site

Myself and my husband Vinny stayed here on our way back from Exmouth with our two small dogs. Sue was lovely as we had drove all day she even stayed up to ensure we were all sorted with our caravan site. Site big enough also nice and flat. Toilets and showers are a little dated but spotless clean. Grounds are maintained well too. 2 mins from the beach. Thanks Sue for ensuring we got here safe!
This park should be absolutely ashamed of themselves - kicking out long term tenants for a money grab!
We enjoy staying here when visiting Geraldton and surrounds. The best view possible.

Out of town so quite peaceful . Toilets clean and very hot water in the showers. Old but well kept.
Park itself is well manicured and the sites lovely and flat.

I would give average because of webs everywhere. Curtains were torn. Pillows were not comfortable. Pool was good and kids enjoyed a lot. The environment was very good. The lady at reception was very nice to us.

Have stayed at this caravan park quite a few time now |This stay was average the cabins are outdated which I was aware of.|The cabin we stayed in was number 36 |Ideal for large family's |The cabin itself was okay |Negatives |No exhaust fan in bathroom |Tv outdated|Remote needed new batteries |No wifi access to cabin|The extra blankets smelt terrible, unusable|No extra bin liners|No extra towels even though booked as a family |Bird poop on outside verandah not clean | Shower head useless as was adjusting the temperature |Toliet in shower room so was hard to be able to use toliet with big family staying there.|Our kids were  also told to be quiet at 3pm in the afternoon by a another camper resident kids are meant to be outside playing ?|No washing machine in cabin |Bbq were great but left dirty |No playground facilities |||Postives|The staff that was on 12.0.24 was very helpful |The rooms were more than enough for a large family|Close to the beach 10 min walk at that.|Carport big enough for both cars

Stayed 4 nights in our caravan at a powered site. Pool and Ablution block only a short walk. Holiday Park is at the top of the escarpment so the view over the ocean is amazing. It faces west so you get to enjoy the sunsets too.|(-) Would welcome a level pad for the caravan. Children’s playground small and old, needs updating. |(+) Sunset view is spectacular.|The swimming pool is crystal clear.
Huge thanks to Pauline who saved me on a wet and wild night. Great location and easy overnighter.

Really enjoyed by stay...the staff were lovely and friendly, the views from the cabin patio of the Indian Ocean were beautiful and the standard of cleanliness was exemplary. Very reasonable prices and close to shops for supplies. Will definitely stay again.
A great park situated a little out of Geraldton. Friendly helpful staff with good clean facilities

We have stayed at Drummond Cove for over 20 years on our way north. this year the powered sites have jumped from $40 to $65 per night. Unfortunately we will not be staying at this price. There are other places in Geraldton that have powered sites for $50.

Dirty old cabins with unwashed blankets.
Dirty old camping toilets with camp kitchen next to rat infested bird cage.
I left in the morning at 6am because our power went out during the night and called office to discuss very civilly some sort of refund only to have my and my wife’s number blocked.
Do not recommend this place, sunset beach park a lot nicer.
Just 15 minutes out of Geraldton. Clean amenities good view to the ocean. Very little road noise even though it’s next to the highway. It’s dated but cheaper than other parks in Geraldton.
Great place to stay in Geraldton

Nice peaceful park, great pool
NEED A RIDE! Local taxi service in Geraldton
https://g.co/kgs/tgDfJfm
Had to make an late and unexpected stop due to car problems. Rang the after hours number and they were great.

Office staff friendly BUT no staff on sites after hours.
Power NOT reliable
Pool area seating requires maintenance along with ablation blocks plumbing
Wouldn't recommend it in present condition.

Close to beach which is the only good thing.
There is a nice pool staff were friendly.
Ablation block clean but needing maintenance grounds are needing the same.
Power continually cutting out and NO staff on site after 5pm with No after hours emergency number this leaves guests and permanent residence with no choice.
Sorry but we won't be back....

Great spot, exactly what we needed! My partener and I stayed in a powered site for 40$/night in our rooftop tent. The site was located in front of the playground and bird aviary and opposite the toilets. Very good if you're fairly self contained. Service was lovely. Would like to see concrete pads for bays and clearer communication regarding sharing bays as we shared with a stranger and both needed access to the power but it all worked out in the end. Toilet/shower facilities clean and as nice as being at home. Lovely spot if youre in Gero!

Specifically booked this place as it had a pool for great for the school holidays.  However the pool was not available for use due to maintenance.  I was not advised of this before we arrived as I could have made alternative arrangements.  Walked in and noticed cockroaches on the kitchen bench, opened the cupboard under the kitchen sink and cockroach ran out from there.  I advised the staff and was given insect spray to use.  Mouse droppings on kitchen floor living area.  Night time there was more little cockroaches crawling on the kitchen bench, stove area, they were coming out from a hole in the wall behind the kitchen sink.  Towels that were supplied were very thin.  Shower head in the shower was very rusty and did not look safe.  Was very disappointed with this place as I paid $170 for this stay.

Great location but cabin was very dated. Needs a serious make-over.
Staff very friendly and helpful.

The good stuff.....||Great location, overlooking the ocean.|Helpful reception arrangements for our late arrival||Not so good.....||Very outdated, needs maintenance and some modernisation.|Evidence of water leaking/stains into ceiling space, rusty shower fitting, old laminex benchtops  loosing laminex, shower extraction fan must have been replaced - square for round, so some gaps in the wall surrounding it.||Worst of all was by nightfall we experienced an infestation of  small cockroaches.  ||When I mentioned this on checkout, to her credit, the lady seemed unaware of any issue and said it would be addressed. Here's hoping....||Would not recommend a stay here unless there is money spent to upgrade it.  Definitely not worth $150 per night.
